headline: Washington tightened its grip on frontier AI as OpenAI and Anthropic gated their newest models behind a government cybersecurity review, while Mozilla showed those same coding agents can be hijacked through a booby-trapped repository.

emerging_trends.text: AI security pressed on two fronts today: the U.S. government now gates OpenAI's and Anthropic's newest models behind a cybersecurity review, and Mozilla demonstrated that indirect prompt injection can turn coding assistants like Claude Code into a developer's own attacker.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Prompt Injection Turns AI Coding Agents Against Developers

emerging_trends.text: Russia's cyber war on Ukraine keeps widening, with Gamaredon mounting 35 spear-phishing campaigns and folding fresh malware and cloud-service abuse into its 2025 onslaught.

Criminal enterprises professionalize even as they draw law-enforcement fire, as Kaspersky exposes The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service operation and the Justice Department seizes nearly 400 World Cup piracy domains.

topics.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Gate New Models Under U.S. Review
topics.latest_developments: OpenAI and Anthropic both confirmed on June 29 that they are limiting their newest models to customers the Trump administration approves while the U.S. government runs a cybersecurity review, a restriction layered onto the GPT-5.6 Sol launch reported earlier the same day.
topics.summary: OpenAI builds ChatGPT and Codex; Anthropic builds the Claude models. Both firms now coordinate releases with Washington, holding back full access for ChatGPT, Codex, and API customers until the review clears.

sources.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Limit New AI Models to Trump-Approved Customers During Cybersecurity Review

topics.title: Prompt Injection Turns AI Coding Agents Against Developers
topics.latest_developments: Mozilla's Zero Day Investigative Network warned on June 29 that a GitHub repository carrying no malicious code can use indirect prompt injection to make AI coding agents such as Claude Code take harmful actions the developer never authorized.
topics.summary: The proof-of-concept hides instructions in repository content that an AI assistant reads and obeys while cloning and setting up a project. Teams should treat cloned repositories as untrusted input and constrain what their agents may execute.

sources.title: Mozilla warns of indirect prompt injection risk in AI coding agents

topics.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware-as-a-Service Surfaces
topics.latest_developments: Kaspersky on June 29 detailed The Gentlemen, a ransomware-as-a-service group, disclosing its custom backdoors, its tactics, and a newly spotted ransomware variant.
topics.summary: The Gentlemen run a rent-the-malware operation that hands affiliates ransomware and custom tooling. Kaspersky's incident analysis maps the crew's backdoors and tradecraft so defenders can spot the early stages.

sources.title: The Gentlemen are knocking: custom backdoors and evolving tactics

topics.title: U.S. Seizes 400 World Cup Streaming Domains
topics.latest_developments: The Justice Department's Criminal Division seized nearly 400 web domains that illegally streamed 2026 FIFA World Cup matches, the agency disclosed on June 29.
topics.summary: The takedown extends a U.S. piracy crackdown that earlier hit the PirloTV sports-streaming network. Investigators pulled the domains offline as the tournament drew global audiences across the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

sources.title: US seizes hundreds of FIFA World Cup illegal streaming domains

topics.title: Gamaredon Expands Its Ukraine Campaign
topics.latest_developments: ESET reported on June 29 that Russia's Gamaredon ran 35 distinct spear-phishing campaigns against Ukraine through 2025, most in the second half, broadening its malware arsenal and abusing legitimate cloud services for delivery and control.
topics.summary: Gamaredon is an FSB-linked Russian advanced persistent threat that has hammered Ukrainian government and military targets for years. ESET's count shows the crew accelerating its tooling and leaning on cloud platforms to evade detection.

sources.title: Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse

topics.title: DirtyClone Linux Kernel Root Escalation
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek disclosed on June 29 DirtyClone, a variant of the DirtyFrag flaw that lets an unprivileged local user manipulate the Linux page cache to seize root privileges.
topics.summary: DirtyClone is a local privilege-escalation bug in the Linux kernel's page cache handling. Any standard account on an affected host can climb to root, so administrators should patch the kernel without delay.

sources.title: 'DirtyClone' Linux Kernel Vulnerability Leads to Root Access

games.recap: Ryan O'Hearn homers twice, helps Pirates avoid sweep with 9-4 win over Reds

business_politics.title: U.S.-Iran Truce Wobbles as Talks Near
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump said June 29 that Iran had requested a meeting, Iranian officials said nothing was scheduled, and both sides accused each other of weekend ceasefire violations ahead of negotiations set for Tuesday, June 30.
business_politics.summary: The United States and Iran agreed to halt days of fighting over the Strait of Hormuz and to resume peace talks aimed at a long-term deal restraining Tehran's nuclear program; markets steadied as Treasury yields rose and gold fell on the prospect of easing tensions.

sources.title: U.S. and Iran Agree to Halt Days of Fighting Over Strait
US says it has agreed deal with Iran to halt strikes and resume talks

business.title: Comcast to Split in Two
business.latest_developments: Comcast announced June 29 it will break into two public companies by spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky, sending shares up about 22 percent.
business.summary: Comcast, the Philadelphia cable and broadband giant whose Xfinity service reaches the Pittsburgh region, will separate its connectivity business from its film, theme-park, and streaming operations, a structural reshaping of one of the country's largest media companies.

sources.title: Comcast plans to split into two public companies by spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky

around_town.title: Heat Dome Bakes Western Pa. Through July 4
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County issued a Code Red heat advisory for June 29 through July 1, with forecasters now showing highs of 93 to 94 degrees peaking Wednesday and Thursday and dangerous heat extending into the July 4 weekend.
around_town.summary: A heat dome covering some 200 million people across the United States will push Pittsburgh-area heat-index values near or above 100 degrees for several days, prompting the county to open cooling centers and warn residents of extreme conditions.

sources.title: Scorching heat wave brings 100-degree feels-like temperatures for 4th of July weekend
A heat dome will have Pittsburgh scorching this week as 200 million across U.S. face extreme temps

around_town.title: Commercial Street Closes Five Weeks for Bridge Slide
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT closed Commercial Street for five weeks starting June 29, marking the start of the long-awaited Commercial Street bridge slide on the Parkway East in July.
around_town.summary: The closure of Commercial Street, along with parts of Forward Avenue and the Nine Mile Run Trail beneath the Parkway East near the Squirrel Hill Tunnel, lets crews slide a new span into place, escalating from last week's testing.

sources.title: Commercial Street closed as Pittsburgh prepares for 376 bridge closure

events.title: Turnstile's Never Enough Tour Comes to Pittsburgh
events.latest_developments: Pittsburgh City Paper reported June 29 that the band Turnstile will bring its Never Enough tour to Pittsburgh, sharing the stage with a genre-spanning lineup of opening acts.
events.summary: Turnstile, the Baltimore hardcore group, plans a Pittsburgh stop on its Never Enough tour with a slate of eclectic openers spanning multiple genres.

sources.title: Turnstile's Never Enough tour is coming Pittsburgh, and they're bringing friends

around_teams.title: Legacy Hall Opens at PNC Park
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed the Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park, a fan space showcasing franchise history.
around_teams.summary: The Pirates built Legacy Hall to let fans young and old walk through the team's baseball history at PNC Park, adding a permanent exhibit to the North Shore ballpark.

sources.title: The Pirates' new Legacy Hall at PNC Park will allow fans young and old to 'see a lot of history'

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Beware of Productivity Paradoxes
reading.summary: Newport argues that powerful new tools like the personal computer promise obvious productivity gains yet often fail to deliver them, cautioning that technology rarely improves output on its own.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Field discusses how he built Figma and why he sees AI as a tailwind that strengthens the company's design tools rather than a threat to them.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches an ongoing series chronicling what he views as an inflating AI and tech bubble, with skeptical analysis of the spending and valuations driving it.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
